summary: The policy document was updated to add explicit indicators for links that open in a new tab, primarily within the 'Dispute Support' section, enhancing user navigation clarity.
analysis:
### 1. What specifically changed

The changes exclusively pertain to the 'Dispute Support' section of the policy document (sourced from `https://www.asbfeo.gov.au/disputes-assistance/dispute-support`). The core 'NSW Government AI Guidance' content (`https://www.digital.nsw.gov.au/policy/artificial-intelligence/generative-ai-basic-guidance`) remains unchanged. 

Specifically, the update involves the addition of the text `(opens in a new tab)` appended to the display text of numerous external hyperlinks. This modification serves as a user interface enhancement, informing users that clicking these links will open a new browser tab or window, rather than navigating away from the current page. Examples include links to: 
*   IDCARE services and forms
*   State-specific Security of Payment Act (SOPA) resources and government planning websites (ACT, NSW, NT, QLD, SA, TAS, VIC, WA)
*   Authorised Nominating Authority (ANA) resources
*   Various state Small Business Commissioners, Tribunals, and Courts
*   ASBFEO online forms

No substantive policy text, legal obligations, or practical guidance related to AI use or dispute resolution procedures were altered.

### 2. Who is affected

This change primarily affects users navigating the 'Dispute Support' section of the policy document, which is designed for small business operators seeking assistance with disputes. Users accessing these specific external links will now have clearer expectations about their browser's behavior. NSW Government staff consulting the AI guidance are not directly affected by this change, as the AI-specific policy content remains untouched.

### 3. Whether this impacts user rights, data privacy, or liability

This update has no impact on user rights, data privacy, or liability. The addition of `(opens in a new tab)` is a purely cosmetic and user experience-driven change. It does not alter the content of the linked external sites, the terms under which those sites operate, the data they collect, or any legal obligations or liabilities for users or the NSW Government.

### 4. Any action required

No action is required from users or internal staff. The change is a minor informational enhancement designed to improve the browsing experience rather than necessitating any policy adherence or operational adjustments.
